name: Google TV
description: Google's smart TV interface and streaming app that aggregates movies and shows from across services on Android TV and Chromecast.

years.notes: Original Google TV platform mark (2010–2014), discontinued in June 2014 when the platform was succeeded by Android TV. Grey TV silhouette with V-antennas and a 3×3 multicolor grid screen (Google-palette tiles). The original Wikimedia SVG also carries a 'Google TV' wordmark below the mark, dropped here because it is illegible at 24×24 and not part of the standalone icon usage. Red sampled from the SVG's brightest red gradient stop (rgb(92.9%,11.0%,14.1%) → #ED1C24); other colors taken from the fetcher palette.

Google Play Movies & TV app icon refreshed in April 2016 as part of the Play family redesign (play-button triangle with film-strip overlay). Used 2016 through 2020 until the app was rebranded as Google TV. Deep magenta Play triangle with two red-gradient film strips and seven white sprocket holes; geometry transcribed verbatim from the upstream SVG into a 24×24 viewBox via a single scale+translate transform.

Current Google TV mark introduced September 30, 2020 alongside the relaunch of Google TV as a Chromecast/Android TV interface and the rebrand of the Google Play Movies & TV app. Rounded TV frame split into four Google brand colors (blue top, green left, yellow bottom, red right) around a white screen. Geometry transcribed verbatim from the Wikimedia Commons SVG (548.3×400) into a 24×24 viewBox; mark sits in a 24×17.5 region with vertical padding (translate(0,3.25) scale(0.04377)).
